title: SELECT, Projection, Expressions, Types, and Result Contracts · Relational Recall
summary: An operations analyst needs a readable, reproducible ticket extract whose columns carry explicit meaning. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a typed ticket projection query pack after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compose SELECT and FROM clauses that project only required columns from a named relation.
Use relation and column aliases to make provenance and output contracts unambiguous.
Build arithmetic, text, date, and CASE expressions while tracing input and output meaning.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Select From · changed case · Review Select From
steps.instruction:
review case — Return unresolved high-or-critical tickets plus unowned work from the seeded database. Expose ticket_id, a readable owner label, age bucket 5, and deterministic severity/opened ordering; then test a NULL owner and tied timestamp.

Which action demonstrates Select From without dropping earlier contracts?

stimulus.title: Distinct Duplicates case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Return unresolved high-or-critical tickets plus unowned work from the seeded database. Expose ticket_id, a readable owner label, age bucket 6, and deterministic severity/opened ordering; then test a NULL owner and tied timestamp.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Duplicate output always proves duplicate stored rows.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use duplicate-preserving and duplicate-eliminating results intentionally and explain their cost and semantics. Artifact under review: a typed ticket projection query pack.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.
Run a convenient Distinct Duplicates query for separate evidence from confidence, omit grain, NULL, cardinality, and dialect checks, then accept one plausible result grid.
The record names the assumption, applies Distinct Duplicates, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use duplicate-preserving and duplicate-eliminating results intentionally and explain their cost and semantics.
